title: National Film Awards 2026 major winners: From Yami Gautam to Kartik Aryan - full list
content: The winners of the 72nd National Film Awards were announced by the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting in New Delhi on Saturday. One of the biggest highlights of this year's honours was Yami Gautam winning the Best Actress award for her powerful performance in Article 370. Sharing the Best Actor honour were Kartik Aaryan for his transformative performance in Chandu Champion and Mammootty for his critically acclaimed role in the Malayalam horror drama Bramayugam.

title: 'Would wake up at noon, hit gym by 5': Yogi's 'babua' dig at Akhilesh's UP CM tenure
content: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath criticized former CM Akhilesh Yadav's tenure. He alleged Yadav lacked public engagement and a development vision for the state. Adityanath highlighted the BJP government's welfare schemes and development projects. He contrasted current progress with the previous administration's perceived failures. The chief minister also accused the opposition of betraying farmers and compromising law and order.

title: 'Fast, unpredictable': What players, coaches said about New Jersey pitch before WC final
content: The hybrid grass pitch at the New York New Jersey Stadium is a major talking point. Players and coaches have shared contrasting views on its speed and firmness. Rain significantly alters the playing surface, making it faster and smoother for matches. FIFA has managed the pitch carefully, allowing it time to recover. The final outcome may depend on Saturday's weather and its impact.

title: 'Only if...': Mehbooba Mufti on NC's J&K statehood protest invite in Delhi
content: Mehbooba Mufti will join the National Conference protest for Jammu and Kashmir statehood.  She demands the agenda also include Article 370 restoration and prisoner release.  Mufti believes protesting solely for statehood legitimizes the revocation of special status.  She urged for an all-party meeting to address disempowering issues affecting people.  Restoring statehood requires sustained political effort and engagement from leaders.

title: Apple targets former employees in battle with Sam Altman's OpenAI
content: Apple has initiated legal actions against OpenAI and its former employees. The company filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of stealing hardware designs and manufacturing blueprints. Apple sent legal warnings to approximately forty former employees to preserve documents. These actions follow a previous partnership between the two technology giants. OpenAI denies the allegations and states no evidence supports the claims.

title: Farm loans with less paperwork? Centre explores digital land record link
content: Digital land records may soon link with Unified Lending Interface for faster farm loans. This integration aims to provide secure, consent-based access to institutional credit for rural citizens. Officials discussed using authenticated digital land records to simplify formal credit access for farmers. The initiative seeks to speed up loan disbursal and lower transaction costs significantly. This plan will promote financial inclusion by using land records as trusted digital assets.

title: 2 held in Guwahati for painting Sonam Wangchuk murals on public wall
content: Two individuals were apprehended for painting activist Sonam Wangchuk murals on public walls.  Guwahati police registered two separate cases against the accused individuals.  Meanwhile, Sonam Wangchuk was shifted to Safdarjung Hospital after his hunger strike.  He completed a twenty-day fast demanding the education minister's resignation.  Police action drew criticism from several opposition parties and student organizations.

title: Airbnb CEO says will keep investing in New York despite Mamdani's rent freeze plan
content: Airbnb and Anthropic are expanding their New York City operations significantly.  This investment occurs despite concerns from business leaders about city policies.  Airbnb purchased a building for its growing New York workforce.  Anthropic will lease a large office building and double its staff.  These expansions highlight continued business activity amidst policy debates.

title: CWG 2026: Mirabai, Lovlina named India's flagbearers for Glasgow opening ceremony
content: Mirabai Chanu and Lovlina Borgohain will lead India's 124-member contingent. They will serve as flag and baton bearers at the opening ceremony. Both accomplished athletes are also strong medal contenders for India. The Indian Olympic Association announced their selection on Saturday. The Games opening ceremony is scheduled for July 23.

title: Human voices near salmon streams made bears and eagles flee, study finds
content: New findings from Canadian researchers reveal that human sounds near Alaska's salmon streams significantly disturb wildlife, leading to grizzly bears avoiding feeding areas. This interference disrupts the natural nutrient transfer to surrounding forests, emphasizing the detrimental effects of human activity on essential ecosystem dynamics. Scientists propose enhanced land management practices to safeguard these critical ecological processes.

title: Scientists fit 75 pronghorns with GPS collars: What they found
content: GPS collars track pronghorn movement near a New Mexico solar project.  Fences around the solar farm present a significant barrier to these animals.  Pronghorn typically crawl under fences rather than jumping over them.  Researchers suggest simple fence modifications could aid wildlife movement.  These changes would help animals use their traditional migration paths.

title: 'Affair, snakebite, Rs 20L insurance': How UP woman, lover plotted husband's murder
content: A woman and her lover were arrested for a planned murder to claim insurance money. They sedated the husband and released a poisonous snake in his blanket. The snakebite killed the man, and the wife initially claimed it was an accident. Investigators found evidence of repeated calls and pictures of a snake. Both confessed to orchestrating the crime with snake charmers for financial gain.

title: Dombivli hospital assault case: Bail of Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre cancelled
content: The Bombay High Court has cancelled bail for Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre and his aides. They must surrender by Sunday evening following an alleged hospital assault. This incident involved attacks on doctors and nurses over patient treatment delays. The Indian Medical Association called for a statewide strike protesting the violence. The court has scheduled the next hearing for July twenty-second.

title: 'Will resign in an hour': Abhishek Banerjee challenges TMC rebels to return to Mamata
content: Abhishek Banerjee challenged former Trinamool Congress colleagues to return to Mamata Banerjee. He stated they would not return as they made a deal with the BJP. Banerjee claimed these individuals received protection from central agencies after switching sides. He mentioned facing multiple summons himself but never running away. This challenge comes amid the Trinamool Congress's significant organizational crisis.

title: Isro targets seven launches this fiscal, next mission set within two months
content: Isro plans at least seven launches during the current financial year, with the next mission scheduled soon. Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1 rocket successfully reached orbit, marking a milestone for India's private space sector. This achievement is seen as a significant step towards the country's "Bharat 2047" vision. Reforms in 2020 have spurred growth, increasing space startups from one to over 400.

title: She called the shots: How 'lady dons' changed over time
content: India's female gangsters have transformed from old mafia empires to younger operators.  These new figures are hyper-local and highly visible on social media platforms.  They often recruit impressionable youngsters and use digital tools to project fear.  This evolution mirrors the changing face of organised crime across the nation.  Law enforcement agencies continue to adapt to these evolving criminal networks.

title: 5 killed, 10 injured after fire breaks out at illegal firecracker unit in Gujarat
content: A major fire erupted at an illegal firecracker unit, killing five people. Ten individuals sustained injuries and were transported to local hospitals for treatment. Senior Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation officials visited the site after the incident occurred. Preliminary investigations suggest the unit was operating without proper authorization. The Prime Minister announced ex-gratia payments for the deceased and injured.

title: Harsh Dubey replaces Sundar as India gear up for ODI decider at Lord's
content: India have added all-rounder Harsh Dubey to their squad for the third ODI against England after Washington Sundar suffered a hamstring injury. With the series tied 1-1, the Lord's decider promises a thrilling finish. India will bank on Virat Kohli's renewed form and hope for KL Rahul's return while seeking their first ODI win at Lord's since 2004.

title: Sold shares, property or crypto? What to know before filing income tax return
content: With the Income tax department increasingly using technology, data analytics, AIS, broker reports, mutual fund disclosures and overseas information exchange, filing an accurate ITR now requires not only correct tax computation but also complete and consistent reporting.

title: Jennifer Winget shares wedding video: Actor posts dreamy white ceremony
content: Actress Jennifer Winget delighted fans by revealing heartfelt moments from her beautiful white wedding ceremony. In a captivating video, she proudly introduced her husband, William Ishmael, a Singapore-based businessman involved in digital trading. The couple celebrated their love in a charming countryside setting in the United Kingdom, receiving warm congratulations from friends and colleagues on their lovely union.

title: 'Filthy air': Trump blames Canada as wildfire smoke clouds Argentina vs Spain WC final
content: Wildfire smoke from Canada is impacting air quality in the United States.  This smoke raises concerns for the upcoming FIFA World Cup final match.  Organisers and meteorologists are closely monitoring the air quality forecasts.  Spain's midfielder noted the visible and palpable haze during outdoor training.  FIFA remains optimistic that the championship game will proceed as planned.

title: 'Football's biggest night': Restaurants, cafes to operate till 4am in Delhi
content: Delhi restaurants and cafes will welcome fans until 4 am this weekend. This decision allows enjoyment of the FIFA World Cup 2026 final match. Defending champions Argentina will face Spain in New York for the trophy. Chief Minister Rekha Gupta announced the extended operating hours for establishments. This move supports the city's 24x7 business-friendly operating framework.

title: 'Sonam's removal wrong': Rahul breaks silence on Wangchuk's protest; slams BJP
content: Rahul Gandhi criticized the Centre's removal of Sonam Wangchuk from Jantar Mantar. Wangchuk was on an indefinite hunger strike for twenty-one days regarding NEET examination irregularities. Police shifted the climate activist to Safdarjung Hospital for medical observation and treatment. Opposition leaders condemned the Centre's actions, calling it suppression of peaceful dissent. The protest demanded resignation and reforms in the education system.

title: Bulldozer action on Trinamool MP Abhishek Banerjee's office in Bengal; police deployed
content: A Trinamool Congress party office in Amtala is being demolished by authorities.  District officials state the five-storey building lacked the required building sanction plan.  Notices were issued and a hearing was scheduled, but no response was received.  Bulldozers are bringing down the structure under heavy security escort.  The demolition operation is being supervised by local administrative and police personnel.

title: Why MHADA Act amendment could be a turning point for Mumbai's ageing buildings
content: Maharashtra's legislature passed a bill to revive Section 79A for dangerous building redevelopment. This amendment clarifies authority for issuing notices under the provision. The law aims to expedite redevelopment of thousands of old and unsafe structures. It empowers tenants to initiate redevelopment if landlords fail to act. The Supreme Court's decision will ultimately determine the implementation of these provisions.

title: 'Nobody can come close to him': Kapil Dev hails Garry Sobers as greatest all-rounder
content: Former India captain Kapil Dev remembered Sir Garfield Sobers as a revolutionary cricketer.  He stated that Sobers changed how future generations approached the sport.  Kapil Dev described Sobers as one of the finest cricketers ever born.  Sobers' influence stretched far beyond statistics and records, he added.  The West Indies great passed away at his home in Barbados.

title: Bharat Future City: How long till you can live in Telangana's futuristic city?
content: Bharat Future City is Telangana’s ambitious plan for a futuristic, net-zero urban centre integrating artificial intelligence, life sciences, advanced manufacturing, healthcare, housing and green infrastructure across southern Hyderabad. Yet, despite attracting 90 real estate proposals, slow approvals, staff shortages and the absence of a final master plan are leaving developers waiting for clarity.

title: 'Dragged, beaten, stripped': Andhra Woman assaulted over water dispute; TDP leader arrested
content: A woman was stripped and assaulted in Guntur over a household water connection dispute. A local Telugu Desam Party functionary was arrested following the shocking incident. Police registered a case against eight individuals involved in the public humiliation. The chief minister condemned the act and ordered stringent legal action. The incident has raised concerns about women's safety and swift justice.

title: India’s leap into the stratosphere: Bridging the gap between drones and satellites
content: India is developing High-Altitude Pseudo-Satellites for enhanced surveillance and communication. These stratospheric platforms offer persistence and flexibility over traditional satellites. HAPS will provide continuous intelligence gathering along India's vast borders. They also serve as vital communication relays for battlefield operations. This advancement strengthens national security and modernizes military capabilities.

title: Buying a plot or a house? Here's which loan is right for you
content: Homebuyers are faced with a decision between land loans and home loans when it comes to acquiring property. Home loans cater to those looking to finance ready homes or their construction, while land loans specifically support the purchase of plots. Though home loans provide higher financing limits and longer repayment periods, land loans usually demand larger down payments and may accompany steeper interest rates.

title: Jani Master faces fresh controversy as Dinesh Master levels serious allegations
content: Dinesh Master, a prominent choreographer, has raised concerns about Jani Master's decision to hire dancers from Mumbai, which he argues adversely affects the livelihoods of local artists in Hyderabad. There are claims that filmmakers are informed of local dancers' unavailability for significant productions while outside dancers come with commission fees. Consequently, the Film Federation has decided to investigate these troubling allegations against Jani Master.

title: 'Heartbreaking to see students suffer': Manu Bhaker speaks out on paper leak protests
content: In a post on social media, the Indian shooter said the issue goes beyond politics and called for accountability to protect the future of students.

title: 'Risk of attacks...': Why Germany raised its security alert to 'high threat level'
content: Germany has elevated its national security threat level to high due to intelligence reports.  Increased intelligence indicates clear plans for attacks targeting the country and its people.  The German Cabinet will consider reforms to expand intelligence agency powers soon.  These reforms aim to allow direct intervention in dangerous situations.  Recent deadly attacks have heightened concerns about national security.

title: CJP yet to seek nod for July 20 Parliament march, say Delhi Police sources
content: High political and security drama continues to grip the national capital. The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) has not yet approached the Delhi Police to seek official clearance for its high-stakes Parliament march scheduled for July 20, police sources revealed on Saturday.

title: Skyroot makes history! 6 payloads put into low-Earth orbit aboard pvt rocket Vikram-1
content: Skyroot Aerospace launched India's first privately developed orbital rocket, Vikram-1.  The vehicle successfully placed four technology demonstration satellites into low-Earth orbit.  This launch marks a significant milestone in opening India's space sector to private companies.  Vikram-1 utilized advanced carbon-composite structures and a restartable liquid-fuelled module.  The mission validates India's capabilities in private orbital launch vehicle development.

title: 'Messi a source of pride for La Masia': Barcelona makes big statement ahead of final
content: Laporta is in New York ahead of Sunday's final at New York/New Jersey Stadium, where current and former Barcelona players will feature on both sides. Spain's squad includes eight Barcelona players, while Messi will captain Argentina.

title: Boyfriend's Marriage Fixed, Heartbroken Techie Dies By Suicide In Mumbai
content: In her last letter addressed to her family, Keerthi wrote that her body be cremated in her boyfriend's native village and that the thali (wedding pendant) be tied to her body.

title: Former Prime Minister Deve Gowda's Wife Chennamma Dies In Bengaluru
content: According to hospital authorities, she was admitted to Manipal Hospital on Old Airport Road in the city on Wednesday night.

title: She Drugged His Milk, Then Released Snake On Him: A Chilling UP Murder
content: The victim, Atul Panwar, who co-ran Krishna Kids Play School in Hastinapur with his wife, Damini, was found dead in his bed.

title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Cost Employers $11.7 Billion In Losses. Here's Why
content: The FIFA World Cup 2026 may have cost the US economy $11.7 billion in terms of lost productivity. Globally, the tournament may have led to a loss of $17 billion in productivity.

title: Meet The Ex-ISRO Scientists Whose Skyroot Sent Vikram-1 Into Orbit
content: Pawan Kumar Chandana and Naga Bharath Daka, both IIT graduates and former scientists at the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO), lead Skyroot Aerospace, India's first space unicorn.

title: Sonam Wangchuk Hospitalised Live Updates: Hospital Says Sonam Wangchuk Refusing IV Fluids, Medication
content: Sonam Wangchuk Hospitalised Live Updates: The action followed a Delhi High Court order for daily health checks on Sonam Wangchuk due to his deteriorating condition.

title: NDTV Probe: CSAM, Sexually Suggestive Content, Gun Culture On Social Media
content: The probe has uncovered a wider pattern of exploitative and illegal content slipping past moderation, raising questions about the consistency and effectiveness of enforcement.

title: Mehbooba Mufti Declines Farooq Abdullah's Invite For Jantar Mantar Protest
content: In a strongly worded two-page letter to Farooq Abdullah, Mufti said that reducing the demand for statehood alone is a betrayal of the people of Jammu and Kashmir.

title: Iran Demands "Blood For Blood" In New Billboard With Trump's Family In Coffins
content: The banner, which has a burning White House as a backdrop, features Trump's five children: Ivanka, Don Jr, Eric, Tiffany, and Barron Trump; and the First Lady, Melania Trump, along with the US president.

title: 2 Arrested For Painting Sonam Wangchuk's Portrait In Guwahati
content: The two men made the paintings in public places without obtaining official permission, which led to legal action, according to police.

title: Sonam Wangchuk Protest Fuels Petition Drive, Social Media Buzz
content: Sonam Wangchuk, a Ladakhi educator and innovator, had been on a hunger strike at Delhi's Jantar Mantar since June 28.

title: Analysis: Why It Is Challenging To Polarise Hindu Voters In Punjab
content: Winning over the Hindu electorate in Punjab has always proved highly challenging. Politicians, barring the hardliners, say they do not focus on Hindus and Sikhs separately. But the reality contradicts their assertion.

title: US Government Employees Now Allowed To Download TikTok On Official Devices
content: In January, TikTok said the venture will retrain, test and update TikTok's content recommendation algorithm on US user data.

title: Skyroot's Vikram-1 Launch Live Updates: "Grand Success," Says Skyroot As Vikram-1 Successfully Releases Payload In Space
content: Skyroot Vikram-1 Launch, Mission Aagaman Live News Updates: The launch is seen as a major milestone for India's rapidly expanding private space sector.

title: Toxic Gas, Bomb Tests: Jaish Terrorists Arrested In Gujarat Took Special Training In J&K
content: The suspects were taught a total of 40 distinct terror techniques, ranging from precision bomb-making to the synthesis of toxic gases using everyday organic materials left underground to ferment.

title: On Camera, Crocodile Drags 12-Year-Old UP Boy Into River, Kills Him In Front Of Uncle
content: The incident reportedly took place on Thursday evening in Muraunwa village in the Baundi police station area, Bahraich.

title: "Those With One Marriage Will Stay In Madhya Pradesh": Mohan Yadav On Uniform Civil Code Bill
content: Madhya Pradesh government to propose Uniform Civil Code bill ensuring legal recognition of only one marriage.

title: Opinion: Reporter's Diary: How Sonam Wangchuk Was Removed From Protest Site
content: There was a sense of uncertainty among the protesters, who appeared anxious but were unaware of what was about to happen.

title: ISRO-SCTIMST sign pact for research in space medicine, Bioastronautics
content: SCTIMST and HSFC have signed an implementing agreement for initiating a PDF programme in Bioastronautics, which is the first programme of its kind in the country

title: Doctors perform complex stenting procedure to treat heart condition in Kurnool
content: Since the patient declined to an open-heart bypass surgery, doctors at KIMS Saveera Hospital perform a highly complex OCT-guided left main bifurcation stenting procedure to treat condition known as triple vessel disease

title: Raichur MP urges corporate houses to be active in CSR activities
content: Companies should send their representatives and experts to the district to study local requirements and demands while releasing the grant under the CSR fund, he says.

title: Chennai hospital gets non-invasive ventilator, pulse oximeters
content: According to a release, these essential tools will strengthen the hospital’s Pediatric Intensive Care Unit (PICU)

title: Two more arrested in Manipur over killing of six Nagas
content: A joint team of the NIA, Manipur Police, and CRPF arrests the two accused from separate villages in Kangpokpi district’s Leimakhong area

title: Death of toddler in Kannur: surgeons’ association picks holes in post-mortem report
content: ‘The remark that no major surgery was required for injuries on the lower lip and the chin is wrong and riddled with errors’

title: NSS terms Mannam Samadhi row ‘politically motivated’
content: Clarification by organisation’s general secretary G. Sukumaran Nair comes in view of criticism from Vice-President C.P. Radhakrishnan and Union Minister Suresh Gopi, who had alleged discrimination in granting permission to pay homage at the memorial of NSS founder Mannath Padmanabhan

content: Since Trinamool Congress’  loss in the West Bengal Assembly election on May 4, Mr. Banerjee has faced criticism from several party leaders for the organisation’s downfall

content: The Deputy Chief Minister is recovering after being discharged from a hospital in Mumbai, where he underwent surgery for a rotator cuff injury

content: Visitors will have the opportunity to experience the latest innovations in the fields through live demonstrations, conference sessions, exhibitor presentations, and dedicated networking opportunities

content: The Minister invites the veteran cricketer, who is also president of the Professional Golf Tour of India, to visit the State for a field assessment of the proposed locations and provide expert guidance on developing infrastructure and tourism circuits

title: A.P. CM inaugurates electric sanitation vehicles for Guntur Municipal Corporation
content: Each vehicle is designed to serve around 1,000 houses, carry 1 tonne municipal waste and operate about 45 km daily for door-to-door garbage collection

title: Former Prime Minister H.D. Deve Gowda’s wife Chennamma passes away
content: She had been admitted to Manipal Hospital in Bengaluru with breathing complications, chest congestion and other age-related complications

content: Naidu lays foundation stones for public health and municipal works worth ₹15,274 crore during Swachha Andhra - Swarna Andhra programme at Gudivada

content: The impact of food adulteration, excessive use of pesticides in farming and counterfeit medicines on the health of people were discussed at a high-level meeting held by Telangana Chief Minister

content: Amal, a DYFI leader, charged with disseminating communally sensitive material. He was later released on bail

title: Ahead of FIFA World Cup final, Kozhikode police announce public safety measures
content: Kozhikode City police said dangerous bike racing, stunt riding, drunk driving, speeding, traffic violations, bursting of firecrackers in a manner that causes public nuisance, raising provocative or hate-filled slogans, and any other activities that disturb public peace have been prohibited

title: Hubballi doctor performs hip replacement surgery on centenarian
content: Although the family had approached various hospitals for treatment, several doctors had advised against going for surgery considering the complications related to his old age.

content: The proposed facility is expected to serve nearly 35,000 people and strengthen healthcare services across nine sub-centres in the Palasamudram area; primary health centres play a crucial role in preventive healthcare, maternal care and immunisation programmes, says Savitha

title: At least four dead, many injured in blast-fire at illegal Ahmedabad firecracker unit
content: The unit, set up on an open farm, was operating without a valid licence, Joint Commissioner of Police Jaipal Singh Rathore said

title: Where football is freedom: A Kolkata mixed-gender team is rewriting rules from margins
content: As the FIFA World Cup commands global attention, children from Kolkata’s Kalighat red-light area are fighting a different battle for the game—one for playgrounds, dignity and the right to dream

title: Ballari: Students protest police action against Sonam Wangchuk, demand abolition of National Testing Agency
content: AIDSO district president K. Eranna alleges that repeated irregularities in examinations conducted by the National Testing Agency, including NEET-UG, had jeopardised the future of lakhs of students across the country.

title: Collector inaugurates new girls’ hostel at Kannan Govt. High School in Chittoor
content: Addressing a gathering, Sumit Kumar says the hostel will provide safe and affordable accommodation for nearly 120 girl students

content: Integrated wildlife management system, rapid-response teams and scientific monitoring proposed for Alipiri and Srivari Mettu footpaths

content: The top court order grants interim relief to residents affected by the ongoing demolition drive targeting mosques, madrasas, dargahs, homes and other structures; court states that no demolition shall take place for two weeks

title: Doctors assault case: Bombay HC stays bail granted to Shiv Sena corporator, asks him to surrender
content: The Bench asked Ramesh Mhatre to surrender before by July 19 evening; said the magistrate court took the issue very lightly and did not even consider his criminal antecedents

content: Experts assess 16th century mandapam and 19th century gopuram, recommend measures for long-term preservation

content: On July 18, Greater Bengaluru Development Minister Krishna Byre Gowda flagged off the drive near Malleswaram in the City Central Corporation limits

content: KSEB plans to complete five BESS projects – two in Kasaragod, one each in Kannur, Malappuram and Thiruvananthapuram – by October.

content: The Defence Minister said India is well-positioned to hit a ₹3 lakh crore defence production milestone by 2029

title: Will join NC protest if Article 370 restoration, lifting ban on Jamaat added to agenda: PDP
content: PDP chief Mehbooba Mufti has set conditions for joining the National Conference’s Delhi protest, seeking statehood, release of political prisoners and a lift on the Jamaat-e-Islami ban

title: Protests rock Dhaka after Hindu man arrested for building 81-ft Lord Ram statue
